tasks.title: Fix path traversal vulnerability in auth import/export
evidence: v1/auth/import and v1/auth/export take a raw 'path' from the request body
This allows reading/writing files anywhere on the disk the process has access to
plan: Modify handleAuthImport and handleAuthExport to validate the path
Restrict paths to a safe directory or use a predefined filename relative to DataDir
Consider removing absolute path support from these endpoints
notes: Fixed path traversal vulnerability in auth import/export
Added validateVaultPath to restrict paths to simple filenames
Paths now resolved relative to DATA_DIR only
Added comprehensive test coverage for path traversal attacks
Updated OpenAPI spec with path validation pattern

tasks.title: Implement RenderWaitModeNetworkIdle in ChromedpFetcher
evidence: ChromedpFetcher.performWait for RenderWaitModeNetworkIdle is a stub (2s sleep)
Robust network idle requires implementing event listeners for network.EventRequestPaused or similar
plan: Implement network activity tracking using chromedp listeners
Replace 2s sleep with a wait loop that checks for zero inflight requests
notes: Implemented network idle detection using chromedp event listeners
Replaced 2-second sleep stub with proper wait loop checking for zero inflight requests
Added comprehensive unit tests (7 test cases) and integration test
All tests pass including CI gate (generate, format, type-check, lint, build, test)
Fixed potential race conditions with atomic operations and channel double-close protection

tasks.title: Implement browser availability check
evidence: fetch.CheckBrowserAvailability is a stub returning nil
healthz endpoint relies on this to report browser status
plan: Implement actual checks for chrome/chromium on PATH
Implement check for playwright drivers if usePlaywright is true
notes: Implemented browser availability check with Chrome/Chromium detection on PATH and platform-specific paths
Added Playwright driver availability check with 10-second timeout protection
Made runtime.GOOS and os.Getenv testable via package-level variables
Comprehensive test coverage for all platforms (Linux, macOS, Windows)
All tests pass and CI gate passes

tasks.title: Capture actual status in ChromedpFetcher
evidence: ChromedpFetcher returns a hardcoded 200 status for all successful captures
It should capture the status of the main navigation response
plan: Use network.EventResponseReceived to capture the status of the main frame request
Update Result.Status with the actual captured status
notes: Implemented responseTracker using network.EventResponseReceived to capture actual HTTP status
Added robust url parsing with net/url for RFC-compliant URL matching
Fallback to 200 with Warn-level log if status not captured (backward compatibility)
18 comprehensive tests covering redirects, ports, query strings, edge cases
Thread-safe concurrent access protection with mutex

tasks.title: Optimize PlaywrightFetcher browser reuse
evidence: PlaywrightFetcher initializes a new Playwright and browser instance for every fetch
This is highly inefficient for concurrent or sequential scrapes
plan: Implement a singleton or pool for Playwright and browser instances
Ensure proper lifecycle management and cleanup
notes: Implemented singleton pattern for Playwright/Browser instances
Browser reused across fetch operations with isolated per-fetch contexts
Added comprehensive test coverage (7 tests including race detection)
Concurrent safety verified with -race flag
Refinement phase: context propagation, cleanup logging, sequential reuse test

tasks.title: Implement pagination in Store.List
evidence: Store.List returns all jobs in a single query
This will scale poorly as the number of jobs increases
plan: Add limit and offset parameters to Store.List
Update API and CLI to support pagination
notes: Added ListOptions with Limit/Offset and Defaults() method
Store.List now delegates to ListOpts for backward compatibility
API /v1/jobs accepts limit (default 100, max 1000) and offset (default 0) query params
CLI 'jobs list' accepts --limit and --offset flags
Bug fix: Store.List now returns rows.Err() instead of nil
Comprehensive test coverage for pagination, defaults, and edge cases

tasks.title: Implement job recovery on Manager startup
evidence: Manager.Start does not load existing 'Queued' jobs from the store
If the server restarts, pending jobs are never processed
plan: In Manager.Start, query the store for jobs with 'Queued' status
Enqueue these jobs into the worker pool
notes: Implemented job recovery on Manager startup with pagination support
Added Store.ListByStatus with pagination options (limit/offset)
Added recoverQueuedJobs method called in Manager.Start
Added comprehensive tests for recovery and pagination
CI gate passes all checks

tasks.title: Unify API error format to JSON
evidence: API error responses use plain text http.Error
The rest of the API returns JSON, creating inconsistency for clients
plan: Create a standard ErrorResponse struct
Refactor error handling to return JSON with appropriate status codes
notes: Added ErrorResponse struct with JSON error format
Added writeJSONError helper function
Replaced 54+ http.Error calls with writeJSONError
Updated OpenAPI spec with ErrorResponse schema
Added comprehensive test coverage for JSON error responses
All API endpoints now return consistent JSON errors

tasks.title: Fix runJobs cancel CLI to be server-aware
evidence: runJobs cancel creates a new Manager instance to cancel a job
This is incorrect if another manager (server) is already running and owns the job
plan: Modify cli.runJobs cancel to check if the API server is reachable
If server is running, use API call to cancel; otherwise fall back to local store update
notes: Fixed cancel CLI to detect and use API when server is running
Removed incorrect Manager instantiation that conflicted with server-owned jobs
Added isServerRunning() helper with 2s health check timeout
Added cancelJobViaAPI() helper with 5s timeout and ErrorResponse parsing
CI passed: all tests green including e2e (22.3s)

tasks.title: Support SchemaInteger in validation
evidence: extract.validateScalar only handles SchemaNumber
SchemaInteger is missing as a explicit type even though it's common in schemas
plan: Add SchemaInteger to SchemaType enum
Update validateScalar to handle integer validation (no decimals allowed)
notes: Add SchemaInteger constant to SchemaType enum
Implement integer validation with math.Trunc for decimal detection
Extract validateNumericConstraints helper to eliminate duplication with SchemaNumber
Add 10 new test cases covering valid/invalid integers, boundaries, and scientific notation
Edge case: Inf without max constraint would be accepted (same as SchemaNumber)

tasks.title: Deep nesting support in Schema validation
evidence: ValidateDocument only handles properties at the top level
It doesn't recursively validate nested objects in properties
plan: Refactor ValidateDocument/validateField to be recursive
Support SchemaObject validation within validateField
notes: Implemented recursive validation with depth tracking
Added SchemaObject support in validateField for nested properties
Added array of objects validation support
Implemented depth limiting (max 10 levels) to prevent stack overflow
Added RawObject field to FieldValue and NewObjectFieldValue helper
Comprehensive test coverage: 7 test functions with 20+ subtests
All CI gates pass: make ci clean

tasks.title: BlockedResourceOther in PlaywrightFetcher
evidence: isBlockedType in playwright_fetcher.go doesn't handle BlockedResourceOther
This makes it impossible to block arbitrary resource types in Playwright
plan: Update isBlockedType to handle BlockedResourceOther
Map it to appropriate Playwright resource types or handle as fallback
notes: Added BlockedResourceOther case to isBlockedType function
Maps to non-essential Playwright resource types (script, xhr, fetch, websocket, eventsource, manifest, texttrack, other)
Added comprehensive test coverage with 28 test cases
Handles literal Playwright 'other' type for beacons, CSP reports, pings
Preserves existing behavior for specific blocked types (image, media, font, stylesheet)

tasks.title: Refactor CLI to reduce duplication
evidence: runScrape, runCrawl, and runResearch have high code duplication for flag parsing and job enqueuing
plan: Extract common flag sets and job enqueuing logic into helper functions
Simplify subcommand implementations
notes: Refactored runScrape, runCrawl, runResearch to use common helpers (registerCommonFlags, resolveAuthFromFlags, initJobManager, handleJobResult)
Refactored runServer to use initJobManager helper (missed in Phase 2)
Refactored runSchedule add to use registerBrowserFlags and registerPipelineFlags
Reduced ~230 lines of duplicated code across CLI commands
All tests pass, CI gate successful

tasks.title: Web UI: Handle multi-line results
evidence: loadResults in App.tsx only parses the first line of the results file
This hides results for crawl and research jobs which produce multiple lines
plan: Modify loadResults to parse all lines of the JSONL file
Update the UI to display a list of results with a details view for each
notes: Modified loadResults to parse all JSONL lines instead of just first line
Added ResultItem union type with CrawlResultItem and ResearchResultItem
Added resultItems state and selectedResultIndex for multi-result navigation
Added isCrawlResultItem and isResearchResultItem type guards
Fixed useEffect to clear stale display state when resultItems is empty
Fixed isResearchResultItem to explicitly check not a crawl result first
Fixed NormalizedView to check if item is crawl result before accessing normalized
Fixed ClusterItem.evidence to use EvidenceItem type consistently

tasks.title: Web UI: Add Cancel Job button
evidence: There is no way to cancel a running job from the Web UI
plan: Add a 'Cancel' button to job items in the UI
Implement the cancel call using the generated API client
notes: Implemented cancelJob function with deleteV1JobsById API call
Cancel button renders for queued/running jobs only
Proper error handling with user feedback
Added 'canceled' case to statusClass for visual feedback
All CI checks pass (generate, format, type-check, lint, build, test)

tasks.title: Web UI: Add Delete Job button
evidence: Jobs cannot be deleted from the system via the Web UI
plan: Ensure API supports job deletion (looks like it does in handleJob)
Add a 'Delete' button to the UI with a confirmation dialog
notes: Added DELETE /v1/jobs/{id}?force=true endpoint
Store.Delete() method with full test coverage
UI Delete button with confirmation dialog
Proper state cleanup (clears selection if deleted)
All CI checks passing

tasks.title: Web UI: Show Manager status
evidence: The Web UI does not show the number of queued and active jobs
This info is available via healthz or a potential new status endpoint
plan: Fetch manager status from the API
Display status in the hero or sidebar of the Web UI
notes: Implemented manager status display (queued/active job counts)
Fetches from existing /healthz endpoint
Type-safe with runtime guards for details field
Polls every 4 seconds alongside job refresh
Displays in hero Live Signals panel

tasks.title: Periodic DB checkpoint in Manager
evidence: SQLite WAL mode is used but checkpoints are only manual or on close
Large WAL files can degrade performance over time
plan: Add a background ticker in Manager to call Store.Checkpoint periodically
notes: Implemented periodic WAL checkpoint goroutine in Manager.Start()
Runs every 5 minutes using PASSIVE mode (non-blocking)
Properly integrates with existing WaitGroup shutdown pattern
CI gate passed: generate, format, type-check, lint, build, test

tasks.title: Cap HTTP fetch response size to prevent memory exhaustion
evidence: internal/fetch/http_fetcher.go reads response bodies with io.ReadAll(resp.Body) and no size limit, so very large responses can exhaust memory.
internal/config/config.go has no setting to cap response size for fetch operations.
plan: Add a configurable max response bytes setting in config + env (.env/.env.example) and plumb it into fetch.Request.
Enforce the limit in HTTPFetcher (limit reader / MaxBytes) and return a clear error when exceeded.
Add tests for over-limit responses and confirm normal-sized responses still succeed.
notes: Added configurable MaxResponseBytes field to config (env: MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES, default 10MB)
Enforced size limit in HTTPFetcher using io.LimitReader with +1 overflow detection
Plumbed MaxResponseBytes through all modules: scrape, research, crawl, jobs, cli, mcp, api
Added comprehensive tests in http_fetcher_test.go covering normal, at-limit, over-limit, and zero-limit (backward compat) cases
All CI passes (generate, format, type-check, lint, build, test)

tasks.title: Stop MCP job manager on Close and use cancellable context
evidence: internal/mcp/server.go NewServer starts the manager with context.Background(), so workers never stop with server shutdown.
internal/mcp/server.go Close only closes the store and does not cancel or wait for the manager goroutines.
plan: Store a cancellable context in the MCP Server and start the manager with it.
Update Close to cancel the context, wait for the manager, then close the store.
Add a test that Close stops the manager and does not leave work running.
notes: Added cancellable context to Server struct (ctx, cancel fields)
NewServer now creates context.WithCancel and passes to mgr.Start()
Close() orchestrates proper shutdown: cancel → Wait() → store.Close()
Added TestServerCloseStopsManager to verify no goroutine leaks
Added TestServerCloseIdempotent to verify safe double-close
All tests pass: go test ./internal/mcp/...
Manager already correctly handles ctx.Done() in workers and checkpoint goroutine

tasks.title: Close HTTP response body on error to avoid leaks
evidence: internal/fetch/http_fetcher.go returns early on err != nil || resp == nil without closing resp.Body when resp is non-nil.
plan: When client.Do returns both err and resp, close resp.Body before retry/return.
Add a unit test using a custom RoundTripper that returns resp+err to assert body is closed.
notes: Fixed response body close on error path (line 87)
Verified all 4 body close paths in http_fetcher.go
Blast radius checked: no similar issues in cli.go
CI gate passed: all tests green including e2e
Test documents edge case; fix verified by inspection

tasks.title: Propagate rate limiter wait errors in HTTP fetcher
evidence: internal/fetch/http_fetcher.go ignores errors from req.Limiter.Wait(ctx, req.URL), so context cancellations do not short-circuit fetch.
internal/fetch/chromedp_fetcher.go and internal/fetch/playwright_fetcher.go have the same bug pattern (discarding limiter.Wait errors).
plan: Check the error return from limiter.Wait and return immediately on cancellation/error.
Add a test that cancels context before Wait and asserts Fetch returns the context error.
notes: All three fetchers (HTTP, Chromedp, Playwright) now propagate rate limiter Wait errors
Added context cancellation tests for all three fetchers
Tests verified with race detector (-race)
Full CI gate (make ci) passes

tasks.title: Make exporter json output real JSON instead of JSONL
evidence: internal/exporter/exporter.go Export() returns string(raw) for both "json" and "jsonl", so "json" output is still JSONL.
plan: Convert JSONL to proper JSON: single object for scrape/research, array for crawl.
Keep jsonl output as-is while fixing json output only.
Add tests validating json output structure per job kind.
notes: Fixed json output to return proper JSON (object for scrape/research, array for crawl) instead of JSONL
Added comprehensive test coverage for all job kinds and edge cases
JSONL format passthrough preserved unchanged

tasks.title: Increase exporter JSONL scan buffer for large lines
evidence: internal/exporter/exporter.go parseSingle/parseLines use bufio.Scanner without Buffer(), so JSON lines >64KB will fail.
plan: Set a larger scanner buffer (and max token size) for JSONL parsing.
Add a regression test with a JSON line larger than 64KB.
notes: Fixed bufio.Scanner buffer in parseSingle and parseLines (exporter.go)
Added scanner.Buffer with 64KB initial buffer, 10MB max token
Added TestParseLargeJSONLLine regression test covering 1KB-500KB lines
Fixed same pattern in e2e requireLineCount helper for consistency

tasks.title: Stabilize exporter field ordering for markdown and CSV
evidence: internal/exporter/exporter.go iterates map fields with range in exportMarkdown and exportCSV, which produces nondeterministic ordering.
plan: Collect field keys, sort them, and use sorted order in markdown and CSV output.
Add tests that assert stable ordering of fields across runs.
notes: Fixed 4 code paths: scrape/crawl markdown + CSV now use sorted field keys
Added 4 regression tests verifying stable alphabetical ordering
All tests pass, full CI gate passes

tasks.title: Stream job results in CLI export/print to avoid large reads
evidence: internal/cli/cli.go runExport reads result files with os.ReadFile, loading entire results into memory.
internal/cli/cli.go printResults also uses os.ReadFile, which is costly for large result files.
plan: Introduce a streaming export path (e.g., io.Reader-based) in exporter.
Update runExport and printResults to stream results from disk.
Add tests covering large result files and streaming output.
notes: Added ExportStream API using io.Reader/io.Writer for memory-efficient large file handling
Updated runExport and printResults to stream from disk using os.Open instead of os.ReadFile
JSONL format now streams with O(1) per-line memory (10MB max buffer vs ~2x file size)
Added 8 new streaming tests covering large files (100KB), error handling, and consistency
Maintained backward compatibility - Export() now wraps ExportStream()

tasks.title: Expose pipeline and incremental options in MCP tools
evidence: internal/mcp/server.go toolsList() schemas omit incremental and pipeline options for scrape/crawl/research.
internal/mcp/server.go handleToolCall creates jobs with pipeline.Options{} and incremental=false, ignoring pipeline/incremental inputs.
plan: Extend MCP input schemas to accept pipeline options and incremental flags.
Parse the new fields and pass them into CreateScrapeJob/CreateCrawlJob/CreateResearchJob.
Add tests covering MCP tool calls with pipeline and incremental options.
notes: Extended MCP tool schemas to accept pipeline options (preProcessors, postProcessors, transformers) and incremental flag
Added getPipelineOptions() helper to extract pipeline options from MCP tool arguments
Updated handleToolCall to parse and pass pipeline options and incremental flags to job creation
Added comprehensive tests: getPipelineOptions unit tests, handleToolCall integration tests, schema validation tests
Verified backward compatibility - new parameters are optional, all tests pass

tasks.title: Return correct Content-Type for job results
evidence: internal/api/server.go handleJobResults uses http.ServeFile without setting Content-Type, so .jsonl results may be served as application/octet-stream.
plan: Set Content-Type based on result file extension (e.g., application/x-ndjson for .jsonl).
Add API tests asserting Content-Type for job results responses.
notes: Added Content-Type header support based on file extension
Implemented contentTypeForExtension helper for .jsonl/.json/.csv/.xml/.txt
Added comprehensive API tests for all file types and edge cases
Store UpdateResultPath helper added for test setup
All CI checks pass

tasks.title: Handle non-OK job result responses in the web UI
evidence: web/src/App.tsx loadResults() calls fetch('/v1/jobs/.../results') and parses response text without checking response.ok, so 404/500 become misleading UI errors.
plan: Check response.ok before parsing and surface server error messages for non-2xx responses.
Introduce a minimal web test harness (e.g., Vitest + Testing Library) and add tests for error states.

tasks.title: Make web API base URL configurable
evidence: web/src/App.tsx calls API helpers with baseUrl: '' and uses fetch('/v1/jobs/...'), so the UI cannot target a non-root API host.
plan: Add VITE_API_BASE_URL (or similar) and wire API client + fetch calls to use it.
Document defaults in web/.env(.example) and add tests covering base URL usage.
notes: Added VITE_API_BASE_URL env var for configurable API base URL
Created web/src/lib/api-config.ts with getApiBaseUrl() and buildApiUrl() helpers
Updated App.tsx to use getApiBaseUrl() for all SDK calls
Added buildApiUrlWithBase() helper to handle trailing slash edge case
Added web/.env.example documenting the new env var
Added 10 tests in api-config.test.ts covering all URL construction cases
make ci passes with all 19 tests

tasks.title: Expand web auth inputs to cover cookies/query token options
evidence: web/src/App.tsx buildAuth() only passes basic and headers, while fetch.AuthOptions supports cookies and query tokens.
plan: Add UI fields for cookies and query params (and map to AuthOptions).
Update buildAuth to include the new fields and add UI tests for serialization.
notes: Added cookies and query params auth inputs to web UI
Created parseCookies() and parseQueryParams() parser functions
Updated buildAuth() to include cookies and query parameters
Added 17 comprehensive unit tests in auth-parsers.test.ts
All CI checks pass (36 tests including 17 new auth parser tests)

tasks.title: Include install step in make ci
evidence: Makefile defines ci as generate format type-check lint build test, but the Makefile contract requires make ci to include install.
plan: Update the ci target to run install before other steps.
Ensure ci still runs generate/format/type-check/lint/build/test in the expected order.
notes: Added install as first dependency in ci target (Makefile:48)
CI gate passes: install, generate, format, type-check, lint, build, test-ci all successful
Implementation is minimal, correct, and idempotent
No other queue tasks affected by this change

tasks.title: Implement real dependency updates in make update
evidence: Makefile update target only echoes manual instructions instead of updating dependencies.
plan: Replace the echo with actual update commands for Go and pnpm deps.
Ensure lockfiles are updated and documented as part of make update.
notes: Replaced stub echo with actual go get -u ./... and go mod tidy for Go deps
Added pnpm update for frontend deps respecting semver ranges
Lockfiles (go.sum, pnpm-lock.yaml) are updated by the tools themselves
Validated: make ci passes cleanly with updated dependencies
Implementation is idempotent and fail-fast (Makefile stops on first error)

tasks.title: Install CLI binary to ~/.local/bin in make build
evidence: Makefile build target only runs go build -o bin/spartan and does not install the binary to ~/.local/bin as required by the Makefile contract.
plan: Extend make build to copy/install the binary into ~/.local/bin (creating the dir if needed).
Keep existing bin/ output for local dev while ensuring the install step succeeds.
notes: Extended build target to install CLI binary to ~/.local/bin (respects XDG_BIN_HOME)
Extended clean target to remove installed binary
Both bin/spartan (local dev) and ~/.local/bin/spartan (installed) are maintained
CI gate passes: make ci completes successfully

tasks.title: Expand make clean to remove lock files and log artifacts
evidence: Makefile clean only removes bin/.data and web dist/node_modules, but the Makefile contract calls for deleting lock files and logs as well.
plan: Add removal for lockfiles (e.g., pnpm-lock.yaml) and common log/artifact directories.
Document any destructive behavior in README or Makefile comments if needed.
notes: Expanded make clean to remove root node_modules, Go test artifacts (*.test, *.out), and log directory (out/stress/)
Added comprehensive documentation clarifying what is removed vs preserved
Preserved source-controlled lock files (go.sum, pnpm-lock.yaml) per standard clean target conventions
All CI checks pass (make ci)

tasks.title: Add missing file-level docstrings to code files
evidence: cmd/spartan/main.go starts directly with package main and has no file-level purpose comment.
internal/exporter/exporter.go and internal/fetch/http_fetcher.go have no top-of-file package purpose comments.
web/src/App.tsx and web/src/main.tsx have no file-level doc comments describing their role.
plan: Scan for code files lacking file-level docstrings (Go + TS/TSX) and add concise purpose comments.
Ensure any executable scripts have --help with examples (confirm or update as needed).
notes: Added package-level doc comments to Go files (main.go, exporter.go, http_fetcher.go, server.go)
Added JSDoc file-level comments to TypeScript files (App.tsx, main.tsx)
All docstrings follow existing codebase conventions
CI validation passes

tasks.title: Fix job cancellation status updates and guard against overwriting completed jobs
evidence: internal/jobs/manager.go: run() returns nil on jobCtx.Err() after setting status to running, leaving jobs stuck in running state.
internal/jobs/manager.go: CancelJob() unconditionally updates status to canceled, even if a job already succeeded or failed.
plan: Update job cancellation flow to set status to canceled when context is canceled and avoid overwriting terminal statuses.
Add tests in internal/jobs/manager_test.go for cancel-after-success and context-cancel status updates.
Run make test and make ci.
notes: Fixed CancelJob to guard against overwriting terminal statuses (succeeded/failed/canceled)
Fixed run() to skip jobs in ANY terminal state, not just canceled
Added explicit StatusCanceled updates when context is canceled in scrape/crawl/research workers
Changed CLI cancel to use manager.CancelJob() for consistency
Added IsTerminal() helper method to model.Job.Status
All tests pass: cancel-after-success, cancel-after-failure, cancel-while-running, and context cancellation
make ci passes with all checks

tasks.title: Delete job artifacts on force delete (result files + job directory)
evidence: internal/api/server.go: DELETE /v1/jobs/{id}?force=true only calls store.Delete without removing result files.
internal/store/store.go: Delete() removes the DB row only; result_path and job directory remain on disk.
plan: Add a store helper to delete job records plus result artifacts and wire it into the force-delete API path.
Cover with tests that create a job + result file then verify cleanup on delete.
Run make test and make ci.
notes: Added DeleteWithArtifacts() method to Store with dataDir field
Modified handleJob() to use DeleteWithArtifacts() on ?force=true
Cancel behavior unchanged - only cancels job, preserves artifacts
Comprehensive test coverage: store-level unit tests and API integration tests
All CI checks pass including make ci

tasks.title: Prevent CLI research panic when --urls is blank or only separators
evidence: internal/cli/cli.go: runResearch uses splitCSV(*urls)[0] without checking length; splitCSV removes empty entries.
internal/cli/cli.go: splitCSV() returns [] when input is only commas/whitespace, causing index out of range.
plan: Guard runResearch against empty splitCSV output and return a clear validation error.
Add tests in internal/cli/cli_test.go for inputs like ',', ' , ' to ensure no panic and proper error.
Run make test and make ci.
notes: Fixed panic by adding validation before urlList[0] access in runResearch and runSchedule
Added TestSplitCSVEdgeCases with 9 comprehensive test cases covering all panic scenarios
All CLI tests pass including new edge case tests
Full CI pipeline passes (make ci)
Manual testing confirms: ',' and ' , ' now return clear error instead of panicking

tasks.title: Centralize request validation across CLI, API, MCP, and scheduler
evidence: internal/api/server.go: validates URL scheme/host, timeoutSeconds, maxDepth, maxPages.
internal/cli/cli.go and internal/mcp/server.go do not validate URL/timeouts/depth/pages before creating jobs.
internal/scheduler/scheduler.go enqueues jobs without validating params.
plan: Introduce a shared validation helper (URL, timeout, maxDepth/maxPages, urls list) and reuse it in CLI, API, MCP, and scheduler.
Standardize error messages and add unit tests for validator plus integration tests in affected packages.
Run make test and make ci.
notes: Created internal/validate package with centralized validation functions
Updated API server to use shared validation for all endpoints
Updated CLI to validate scrape, crawl, and research parameters
Updated MCP server to validate tool parameters
Updated scheduler to validate schedule parameters in Add function
Added comprehensive unit tests for validation functions and request validators
Added integration tests in API and scheduler packages
All CI checks pass: make ci successful

tasks.title: Surface scheduler auth resolution errors instead of silently ignoring them
evidence: internal/scheduler/scheduler.go: enqueue() assigns authOptions, _ := loadAuth(...) and discards errors.
internal/scheduler/scheduler.go: loadAuth() can return errors (invalid profile, resolve failure) but they are ignored.
plan: Propagate loadAuth errors and ensure schedules surface failures (log + skip or mark schedule error state).
Add tests for invalid auth profile to verify failure handling.
Run make test and make ci.
notes: Propagated loadAuth errors for all three schedule kinds (scrape, crawl, research)
Added slog.Error logging when enqueue fails in Run()
Added table-driven test TestEnqueueAuthResolutionFailure covering all schedule kinds
Simplified test helpers by replacing custom functions with strings.Contains()
Renamed store type to scheduleStore to avoid naming conflict with internal/store

tasks.title: Add schedule CLI flags for extract config, incremental mode, and auth overrides
evidence: internal/cli/cli.go: runSchedule add only supports auth-profile + pipeline + browser flags; no extract/incremental/auth overrides.
internal/scheduler/scheduler.go: loadExtract reads extractTemplate/extractValidate and enqueue reads incremental but schedule params never set them.
plan: Extend schedule add flags to capture extract-template/config/validate, incremental, and auth overrides (headers/cookies/tokens/login).
Ensure scheduler loadExtract/loadAuth consume these params and add tests for scheduled job param persistence.
Run make test and make ci.
notes: Added extract flags: --extract-template, --extract-config, --extract-validate
Added incremental mode flag: --incremental
Added auth override flags: --auth-basic, --token-kind, --token, --header, --cookie, --login-*
Fixed critical bug: flag registration functions now return pointers (repeatable flags work correctly)
Added comprehensive tests: TestExtractConfigPersistence, TestIncrementalModePersistence, TestAuthOverridePersistence
Scheduler now loads extract config files and auth overrides from schedule params

tasks.title: Expose schedule management endpoints in the API
evidence: internal/api/server.go: Routes() has no schedule endpoints.
internal/cli/cli.go: runSchedule provides list/add/delete; scheduler exists but API has no access.
plan: Add /v1/schedules list/add/delete handlers that wrap scheduler.LoadAll/Add/Delete with validation.
Update api/openapi.yaml and regenerate clients; add API tests for schedule CRUD.
Run make test and make ci.
notes: Added schedule CRUD endpoints (GET/POST/DELETE /v1/schedules)
Modified scheduler.Add to return added schedule for correct POST response
Comprehensive test coverage (5 test functions, all passing)
CI passes completely
Fixed critical bug in schedule retrieval
